review_title: A solid remote access option while others get worse.
description: I came into a team that was using a single Parsec account to manage multiple systems which is not a good place to be in. In a previous job I had done a similar deployment with Teamviewer only today to find out they have really dropped the ball. Splashtop hit the budget value and the feature requirements and it was easy to get rolling. My team from technicians to salespeople was able to "figure it out" quickly and now they wonder what we'd do without it.
author: James L.
pros: Obviously it works. The connections are fast, plenty of options (that actually work, turning the speed option on actually makes a difference on a slow connection). So far support has been fine but a good note from me is I haven't needed to reach out to support all that much, things work. The management system is also straightforward, was able to get up and running quickly. Also the price is good value and something I did like about the company is they simply don't offer a "free" option thus sidestepping the ability for scammers to use the system. Oh and they also really only do remote access and not a dozen other things. They seem focused on core business and I appreciate that.
cons: The deployment system is a little sparse, only a few options. Also would prefer is SSO was not gate-kept behind the Enterprise version. The biggest issue I had was just buying the thing, the purchase page is a little confusing with different options that overlap but makes you feel like you need more than you do.

review_title: No Fuss, Highly Customizable, Excellent Value!
description: From the initial test drive to deployment, customer service has been wonderful and I was able to get this software out to all the devices in my organization without any issues. I am looking forward to utilizing the add-ons, especially the software updates.
author: Brian S.
pros: My company loved the price, yet I love the capabilities. Its so easy to deploy, and the customization is wonderful. I am a very organized person and the computer management options are everything I need. This software checks all the boxes.
cons: I would like to be able to switch screens on the device I am remoted into easier. There is only one button to switch and some of my customers have multiple monitors I need to flip through.
author_duration: Used the software for: Less than 6 months

review_title: So happy I moved over to Splashtop!
description: Great, it has never dropped me and it's simple to understand and easy to use. I find it very reliable indeed. Speed of connection is excellent.
author: Julianne W.
pros: Very easy to set up and use. No drops in service and stays connected very well. I have had no issues at all since joining Splashtop and it out performs my previous service incredibly. I am so happy I have this service and after 2 months of use I have no complaints!
cons: I wish I could minimize the centered "top task bar" when connected because it obscures my ability to minimize or close windows when using a split screen.
author_duration: Used the software for: Less than 6 months

description: It was fairly wonderful upon initial implementation (after GoToAssist after being railroaded into GoToRemote and telling us they were sunsetting the product) then it went from good to bad to worse after update iterations. In the last month it has become nearly unusable by a many of us. It would either not connect unless you are patient to wait a few minutes, close it, start it, and repeat the process between 2 to 6 times. Then after a little while of using, it would become unresponsive or literally crash on you and close all existing session and you'd have to start all over again. A major source of frustration when you are under pressure to resolve issues remotely. Their support became bad with time.
author: Daniel B.
pros: It's easy to deploy. The interface is smooth. It has wonderful features. It's fairly easy to manage.
cons: It's CPU intensive on both the client and technician device (so far the worse we've seen, higher than GoToAssist that is/was Java based). Since their latest update 3.8.0.1 it has gotten worse. It has become difficult to connect to a remote device (you get the circling, formerly hourglass, image going forever until you close the window and try another 2 to 6 times before connecting or giving up depending on the individual). It crashes so often that, internally, it has become known as Crashtop. Even after shelling out several thousands of dollars management has decided to eat their loss and move to another platform as they were facing a revolt by technicians claiming they would go on sick leave rather than having to use the tool. I'm sure, as you know, your mileage will vary. Here ==> it has been ostracized. Their support used to be decent but as of late it became rather useless. You'd get the get same canned answers as if you'd be corresponding with a chatbot more or less expecting this level of support.

review_title: Outstanding support and an exceptional customer experience
author: Rodrigo M.
pros: Outstanding support and an exceptional customer experience Honestly, I never imagined I would have this level of access and attention from the Splashtop team.

description: Very reliable with minimal issues. It's difficult to recall a time when there was an outage for the product.
author: Verified Reviewer
pros: Better pricing model than the competitor we were using. The options available were also not as limitied.
cons: We have run into some issues on some laptops that go to sleep. The SplashTop agent is running but you cannot connect. You have to close the agent on the remote computer and reopen it.

description: Very pleasant - helps make my job a lot easier when a script doesn't cut it and when I won't be able to walk a client over the phone through a fix. Remoting in is a necessary tool for any IT provider but this remote tool specifically feels much cleaner and provides a smoother connection than others I have used - not as buggy and provides feedback when things go wrong.
author: Andrew W.
pros: Very simple to use, convenient tools that make it so I can perform remote IT effectively. The ability to swap between admin controls when things get serious and ease of management are the most valuable tools.
cons: At the moment, the only complaint I would have is the way multiple monitors are handled. In Splashtop business I can setup a client with 3 monitors in 3 windows for easy use, but in the RMM app I have to swap between monitors one by one.
author_duration: Used the software for: Less than 6 months

description: It is a very easy app to use and understand. It is much more reaponsive than the last remote service that I used
author: Sandra H.
pros: I like the clean and easy look of Splashtop as well as the responsiveness of the site. It is easy to use
cons: I am just learning about how to print from my remote computer to the one that is near me. There is a bit of a learning curve
author_duration: Used the software for: Less than 6 months

description: We use Splashtop for access to our building controls system and we can access it from anywhere in the world.
author: Dave L.
pros: You get real value with splashtop business for your money, it is very reasonable for a yearly subscription and have never had a problem with billing.
cons: Not a lot of complaints for this product, my subscription allows 3 computers on at once, they could add a couple more users. 3 users is still a good deal.

review_title: Reliable and Efficient Remote Access Tool
description: My overall experience with Splashtop has been extremely fulfilling. The tool ensures we offer remote assistance quickly so our remote employees do not face any downtime.
author: Vanessa C.
pros: Splashtop has highly proactive and responsive clients support team that offers helps 24/7 so our team do not experience downtime. I like that it has a clean and intuitive interface that is very easy to navigate even for new users without any technical skills.
cons: I have not experienced any challenges with Splashtop. It has been a seamless tool for us. NO dislikes.

review_title: Splashtop - My Favorite Unattended Remote Access Program
description: The overall ease of installation and use is refreshing. The deployment package for Splashtop Streamer is easy to create and install, the UI is less cluttered because they're not trying to do too much. I wish there were a way to have the Splashtop Business app to start with Windows like the Streamer does, but honestly I can't say enough good things about Splashtop.
author: Matthew C.
pros: I switched to Splashtop from TeamViewer for unattended remote access, and I couldn't be happier. The price is hundreds of dollars cheaper per year, and the functionality and ease of use is comparable. Creating a deployment package was straightforward, and the SOS app for attended sessions is light and easy to have remote users run.
cons: My only gripe is that there is a very slight delay in input to a remote computer compared to TeamViewer. It does not impede my work, and the price trade-off is worth the slight delay honestly.
author_duration: Used the software for: Less than 6 months

description: I've used it for years and it's been great. I used to use TeamViewer but not anymore.
author: Doug D.
pros: Spashtop is simple, straightforward and effective.
cons: I have no complaints in my use of Spashtop.

description: It was easy to set up my remote clients. They actually did it themselves, and there was no downtime.
author: Judi J.
pros: As a long time TeamViewer user, I find Splashtop pretty awesome. It does everything TV does (that I need).
cons: Nothing yet. It's working very well for everything.
author_duration: Used the software for: Less than 6 months

description: Super easy to get connected once app is loaded on all devices. Having access to my desktop at any time from any location makes working away from the office the best part of this application.
author: Michelle S.
pros: The ability to access my desktop computer from any location.
cons: Resolution on laptop not the best at times, but I believe splashtop offers a version higher than the one I purchased that has better resolution. So this is a fixable problem.
author_duration: Used the software for: Less than 6 months

description: Splashtop works very well and has a bunch of features i find usefull such as two factor authentication which guarantees me my connections are safe.
author: David M.
pros: Splashtop is supported on windows, mac and even linux and as so i can use it on any laptop or computer we have at the company. Splashtop also offers a free trail.
cons: Remote printing on Splashtop sometimes does not work so well and needs a few tries to work.

description: Overall Splashtop is a gamesaver for me on many occasions when I need remote access to my work computer while on the toad.
author: Art W.
pros: Being able to remotely access my office and home computers is vital to my business.
cons: It would be nice if we could have two simultaneous remote connections as part of the standard subscription.

description: I get to literally work from home! and I can prepare items for transfer to devices that I don't physically have access to like documents to the big copier/printer etc.
author: Phyllis S.
pros: Splashtop is very critical to my success working remotely from home. Without Splashtop, I would have to commute to the building more than once a week. It is very user-friendly.
cons: I recently experienced difficulty connecting with Splashtop using a laptop computer. I don't know if it was settings on the computer or just bad wifi. I was able to do the work using my phone so all was not lost and this is another of the features that is most handy... access using mobile devices, particularly cell phones.

review_title: Use Splashtop to Facilitate Remote Access, and Presentations
description: I have a few computers that I have set up permanent remote access to. For these computers, I can initiate remote access in an unattended state, or without anyone's permission. This is very useful for computers I can administer remotely. I uses the SOS tool on an as-needed basis to do one time support sessions to help a user with a hardware or software problem. I also use the SOS tool to share my screen and present ideas, or show project progress.
author: Clifton S.
pros: Splashtop gives me the ability to take my computer expertise and use it to help others. It also gives me the ability to present ideas and to demonstrate designs without the need to meet in person. It allows a collaboration between all the information on both the sender's and the user's computers.
cons: It can be challenging for the non-savvy computer user to download and install the SOS app. I usually have to walk them through the process. I don't see where this process could be simplified, I just see some of my clients struggle with it. I think it is a great security feature, forcing them to be fully involved in the decision making process of allowing remote access to their computer.

description: Really like that it's light weight, not a resource hog, and gives us high quality resolution so that we can quickly and securely remotely manage endpoints.
author: Paul A.
pros: Allows us to have permanent remote access. This is especially helpful when working with unattended devices. No need to keep installing the packages.
cons: Can be laggy if the internet signal is weak. Some OS updates can uninstall it.

description: It works really well for the few hours a week I can't be present in my office.
author: desiree C.
pros: I love the ability to work productively from home.
cons: Nothing to report that I feel needs improvement

description: Splashtop was relatively easy to use for solving an internal network access issue with my employer and seemed easy enough for IT to set up
author: Kaila C.
pros: It was convenient for resolving connection issues between internal networks for employees to ease access
cons: Splashtop was a little bit of a Learning Curve, and sometimes had connectivity hurdles/limited access

review_title: High quality remote desktop access tool!
description: Overall a great experience! Virtually lag-free remote desktop access and a fairly robust preferences panel helps me set up the security I need as well as allowing the IT dept. to help me out.
author: Johnny P.
pros: It's super great to use as a remote desktop tool. There are little to no slowdowns when accessing my computer remotely. I'm able to get assistance from IT quickly and easily through Splashtop.
cons: It's a pretty solid software, there's nothing really that I would improve. I had to go into the settings a few times when setting it up, so maybe some intentional default settings would be good.

description: I’ve been using it as a support tool for about a month without any issues. It allows me to help an elderly aunt in a neighboring town without having to rush over to her home
author: Thomas D.
pros: Ease of use . Cost factor. Always works . Not overly technical knowledge needed to use
cons: So far nothing comes to mind . The product works as described
author_duration: Used the software for: Less than 6 months

description: The business version also offers many advantages, it can be installed on any device without any additional cost. This allows me to provide support whether I'm in the office, at home, or on the go. Another feature I like about splashtop is that it allows multiple users to provide support simultaneously.
author: Robert M.
pros: I have been using Splashtop for remote access for a long time and its connection is fast and covers all operating systems including mobile. With the increasing mode of remote work, we need unattended access to a variety of devices like servers and phones, and Splashtop does this very well. We even switched from other popular software purely for the cost advantage, it's cheap compared to the competition. A standout feature is the ability to brand the single login applet, allowing us to maintain brand consistency.
cons: Although it works well with the tools it has, splashtop needs to add more functions that allow it to have a more dynamic connection, the UX needs improvement, the order of the menus needs a better order, sometimes it is difficult to find a function, another aspect What confuses is the name of its clints, instead of being a single installation for all the devices, you have to choose whether it is to manage or receive connections.

description: I would give my overall experience as Excellent. You solved a major issue of accessing my computers withoug having to move them all to one location.
author: Beverly O.
pros: The ability to print locally when I am accessing my computer remotely is the best feature by far.
cons: There is nothing at the moment I would improvie upon. I have recommended Splashtop to several friends who have signed up.
